category: stale meta description contradicting current page body

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text. Product title, roaster name/location, origin, region, process, varieties, producer, altitude, harvest, roast level, tasting notes, descriptive text, default 250g size, $22.00 USD price, package grams, one-time purchase context, and in-stock availability are all explicitly present or directly entailed. 'is_specialty_coffee' is supported by the specialty roaster/product context. 'is_blend', 'is_decaf', and 'is_coferment_or_infused' are supported by the single-origin-style presentation and absence of decaf/infusion/co-ferment indications. 'is_espresso': false is supported because the page frames espresso only as a brew recommendation option ('also makes a juicy... espresso'), not as an espresso-designated product.

realism.reasons:
This is quite plausible as an html-to-text rendering of a real specialty coffee product page. The structure is very believable: navigation, breadcrumb, product title, price, size/grind selectors, stock note, tasting notes, origin/process details, descriptive copy, brew recommendation, shipping note, variant block, and footer all match common Shopify-style roaster pages. The coffee details are coherent and specialty-realistic: Ethiopia/Sidama/Bensa/Bombe, Damo Station, natural process, JARC varieties, high elevation, seasonal harvest timing, and flavor notes like peach, blueberry jam, jasmine, and cacao nib fit the profile well. The production description about floating and raised-bed drying for 18–22 days also sounds authentic.

The main tell is the metadata mismatch: the meta description and OG:title refer to a completely different coffee, Guatemala La Colina, which is a noticeable artifact. That kind of stale SEO metadata can happen on real sites, but it stands out. Another minor tell is that the visible grind selector only shows Whole Bean while the variant data includes Ground for Filter, suggesting the text extraction may have dropped an option or the page content is slightly inconsistent. The variant/SKU block itself is synthetic-looking in presentation, but the actual contents are internally consistent and realistic for ecommerce back-end data. Overall, this reads very close to a real roaster page with a couple of small inconsistencies.

label.coffee.sensory_text:
Peach, blueberry jam, jasmine, cacao nib

In the cup we find a layered natural profile: ripe peach up front, blueberry jam sweetness, a soft jasmine aroma, and a clean cacao nib finish. We roast it light to preserve the florals and keep the fruit bright rather than boozy.
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: Peach, blueberry jam, jasmine, cacao nib
label.coffee.producer_text: This lot comes from smallholder growers around Bombe village in the Bensa district of Sidama. Cherries are delivered to Damo Station on the day of picking, floated for density, and dried slowly on raised beds for 18-22 days with careful turning through the first week.
